description:
[SPLIT 2026-05-26 — SUPERSEDED BY four sequenced successors: TASK-081 (pi-jit-agents element — additive build inside pi-jit-agents, spec.tools wired into executeAgent grant + clamp), TASK-084 (POC: orchestrator → pi-jit-agents direct dispatch end-to-end; runs after 081), TASK-082 (pi-workflows consume canonical + delete duplicate; runs after 084), TASK-083 (pi-behavior-monitors consume canonical + delete duplicate; runs after 082). status=cancelled (tasks schema has no 'superseded' status enum value); the four successors carry the actual scope. Original bundled-scope description preserved below as historical record.]

--- ORIGINAL DESCRIPTION (historical, pre-split) ---

FEAT-001 — pi-workflows + pi-behavior-monitors CONSUME pi-jit-agents' WHOLE agent layer; delete pi-workflows' duplicate. Re-scoped 2026-05-26 from the original type+loader-only cut (which was wrong — loader, templates, schemas, compile, and capability are one coupled concern). jit-agents owns AgentSpec + loader + compile (templates/macros/schemas/contextBlocks, spec-dir-relative resolution) + capability composition (spec.tools threaded to the dispatch grant per DEC-0047: operation-granular, default EMPTY, clamped child-subset-of-parent at operation scope). pi-workflows DELETES its duplicate: local AgentSpec type, agent-spec.ts loader, the agent parts of template.ts (the separate .pi/templates + templates/ roots), compileAgentSpec, separate agent-schema resolution. pi-workflows becomes orchestration-only (DAG, state, expressions, gates); a workflow step that needs an agent dispatches a jit-agent directly (per JI-021/010: orchestrator uses jit-agents directly; JI-001: agent is one thing whether monitor or workflow-used; FEAT-001 issue-043 duplication closes). pi-behavior-monitors repoints to jit-agents (drops the synthetic CompiledAgent build). Per DEC-0048: existing .workflow.yaml + bundled agents + their tests are NOT targets (disposable legacy predating pi-context/pi-jit-agents); a broken bundled-artifact test is not a regression gate; engine UNIT tests remain gates. Per DEC-0047: capability-widening writes require writer.kind=human; dispatch threads spec.tools as the explicit grant and clamps child-subset-of-parent (FGAP-099 subsumed here). Validation throughout = REAL CHECKS (build/check/test exit codes, runtime demo, adversarial on external artifacts) — never LLM self-report (FGAP-102 / DEC-0047 clause 5). Aligns with narrowed DEC-0044 (commit a71c782) + closed FGAP-112 (commit 71a7ea2): the original 'new dedicated agent-dispatch extension wrapping pi-jit-agents' framing was narrowed to 'new pi-agent-dispatch extension whose ONLY scope is the sub-agent→sibling-agent pi.registerTool site; jit-agents stays library imported directly by orchestrator + workflows + monitors.' The user-intent framing this task is built on (JI-021/010: orchestrator uses jit-agents directly) is now CANONICAL via the narrowed DEC-0044, not in conflict with it. The pi-agent-dispatch extension itself is downstream FEAT-004/005 work, not part of this task's scope.
acceptance_criteria: pi-jit-agents AgentSpec + loader + compile + agent-template/macro/schema resolution are the SOLE agent layer; pi-workflows consumers import from @davidorex/pi-jit-agents
pi-workflows DELETES: local AgentSpec type, agent-spec.ts loader, the agent parts of template.ts (.pi/templates+templates/ separate-roots), compileAgentSpec, separate agent-schema resolution
pi-workflows is orchestration-only; a workflow step needing an agent dispatches a jit-agent directly (per JI-021/010)

pi-jit-agents WIRES spec.tools into the dispatch grant: operation-granular, default EMPTY (no inheritance of full defaults), clamped child subset of parent at operation scope (FGAP-099 subsumed here per DEC-0047)
pi-behavior-monitors imports agent infra from pi-jit-agents (not pi-workflows); the synthetic CompiledAgent build dropped

Per DEC-0048: bundled .workflow.yaml + bundled agents + their tests are NOT preserved/aligned/fixed; failures of legacy-artifact tests are NOT regression gates; engine UNIT tests (pi-context/pi-jit-agents/pi-workflows engine behavior) remain gates

VALIDATION = real checks: build/check/test exit codes (no pipe-masking), runtime demo (actual feature path exercised end-to-end), adversarial check on external artifacts (git diff / event stream); never LLM self-report (FGAP-102 / DEC-0047 clause 5)
engine build/check/test green at every commit (husky); runtime demo + fresh-context adversarial audit per DEC-0018

Aligns with narrowed DEC-0044 (a71c782) + closed FGAP-112 (71a7ea2): orchestrator + workflows + monitors consume pi-jit-agents as shared library directly per the narrowed DEC-0044; the pi-agent-dispatch extension is downstream FEAT-004/005 scope, not this task.
notes: Re-scoped 2026-05-26 from the original loader+type-only cut. Authority: analysis/2026-05-26-jit-agents-user-intentions.jsonl (39 verbatim USER intentions — esp. JI-001 uniform-agent definition; JI-002 disposability; JI-010 jit-agents owns spec-to-typed-result; JI-021 orchestrator uses jit-agents directly; JI-023/024/028/029 capability/perms operation-granular from empty; JI-027 "validate not fake validate"). Grounding: analysis/2026-05-26-project-vs-jit-intentions-eval.jsonl (conflict EV-001: DEC-0044 new-extension never user-stated; orphan EV-020: an out-of-canon term flagged as never-user-said — purged in 4b8508e/3187ff6). External prior art: analysis/2026-05-26-pi-subagents-eval-vs-jit-intentions.md (the grant mechanism is `pi --tools` at the subprocess boundary — small thread; opt-in-from-empty default; depth-clamp pattern generalizes to tool-clamp). DEC-0044 narrowed + FGAP-112 closed 2026-05-26 (a71c782 / 71a7ea2): canon-vs-intent conflict resolved; orchestrator-direct-use is now canonical. NO RELEASE (held). SPLIT 2026-05-26 per user direction (focus is pi-jit-agents element first + POC; consumer cascade follows): TASK-080 is split into three sequenced tasks + the POC. Successors: TASK-081 (pi-jit-agents element — additive build in pi-jit-agents only; spec.tools wired into executeAgent grant + clamp per DEC-0047; no consumer touching), TASK-082 (pi-workflows consume canonical + delete duplicate; depends on TASK-081), TASK-083 (pi-behavior-monitors consume canonical + delete duplicate + drop synthetic-CompiledAgent bridge; depends on TASK-081 + TASK-082). The POC at TASK-084 (orchestrator → pi-jit-agents direct dispatch end-to-end) runs between TASK-081 and TASK-082 to validate the architectural shape before consumer cascade. TASK-080 itself is cancelled as an umbrella — work proceeds through the four successors. All four carry the canon-aligned framing (narrowed DEC-0044 a71c782 + closed FGAP-112 71a7ea2: orchestrator + workflows + monitors consume pi-jit-agents as shared library directly).
